title: Sony announces end of PlayStation discs, parts of digital store in the same day
gist: Sony will stop producing physical PlayStation discs as of January 2028, moving to a digital licensing-only model as digital hit 78% of full-game unit sales, while simultaneously closing PS3 and PS Vita stores, raising game-ownership and preservation concerns.

insights.claim: Sony will stop producing physical PlayStation discs as of January 2028, after which new games sell in digital formats only, marking the company's full evolution toward a licensing-only sales model.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: A digital-only model captures full storefront margin and eliminates manufacturing/retail costs but concentrates platform control and regulatory/consumer-rights risk.
insights.quote: new games will be available on PlayStation Store and at retailers in digital formats only
insights.locator: Some gamers are concerned about the future

insights.claim: Digital downloads reached 78 percent of Sony's full-game unit purchases in the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, up from 76 percent in fiscal 2024, the consumer-trend data Sony cites to justify ending discs.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: A slow 2-point annual climb suggests physical demand persists at ~22%; ending discs is a deliberate strategic push, not a passive response to fully-collapsed demand.
insights.quote: digital downloads accounted for 78 percent of full-game unit purchases, up from 76 percent in fiscal 2024
insights.locator: During Sony’s fiscal year ending on March 31, 2026

insights.claim: Sony's own subsidiary Sony Digital Audio Disc Corporation is the sole manufacturer of PlayStation discs, so its decision unilaterally ends physical PlayStation copies with no third-party fallback.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: Vertical control over disc production means the transition is irreversible and entirely Sony-timed; no supply chain can sustain physical media independently.
insights.locator: No companies other than Sony subsidiary

insights.claim: Under PlayStation's terms of service, buying a digital download grants only a personal, non-transferable license and customers 'do not own the product,' meaning purchased games can legally be removed from libraries.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: License-not-ownership terms are the structural consumer-rights fault line as physical fallback disappears; a magnet for future regulation and litigation.
insights.quote: you buy a personal license to use that product for private, non-commercial use
insights.locator: However, buying a digital download is not the same

insights.claim: Sony will close the PlayStation 3 and PS Vita stores with US access ending July 2027, while declining to commit to keeping previously purchased games downloadable for customers' lifetime.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: The refusal to guarantee lifetime downloads on shuttered stores is the concrete precedent that undermines confidence in a disc-free future.
insights.locator: Furthering this concern, Sony also announced

insights.claim: Sony has repeatedly revoked purchased digital content, deleting Funimation libraries in 2024 despite 'forever' promises and pulling StudioCanal titles in Germany, Australia and soon the UK, establishing a pattern of temporary digital libraries.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: Documented revocation history means library-loss risk is empirical, not hypothetical, weakening the value proposition of digital-only purchases.
insights.locator: Sony has repeatedly reminded PlayStation customers

insights.claim: After Nintendo 3DS and Wii U storefronts closed in 2023, the number of Game Boy-era games still available fell from 155 of 1,873 to 25, per the Video Game History Foundation, illustrating how storefront closures erase digital-only titles.
insights.speaker: Scharon Harding
insights.so_what: Hard data quantifying preservation loss from store closures; a systemic risk to game IP longevity in a disc-free industry.
insights.locator: After those digital storefronts closed in 2023
